title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) for standardized agent-to-data connections. Covers building agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB with integrated observability for performance and cost tracking.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech session covers building agents with the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and connecting them to data sources through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), which standardizes agent-to-data connections. The demonstrated agents access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB, with observability integrated for tracking performance and cost. The standardized MCP layer matters because it reduces custom integration work across multiple data backends, and the AgentOps focus addresses operational concerns of running agents in production.
